nodes.label: Whitley Strieber (extended)
nodes.summary: American author; 'Communion' (1987) defined modern alien-abduction mythology and imagery of the Grays. Currently has alleged alien implant in body that activates every 2-3 years. Uncle (Mickey) worked with General Exxon at Air Material Command in 1947.

sources.quote: Author of Communion; canonical abductee experiencer; implant carrier

nodes.label: David W. Webb
nodes.summary: NASA-adjacent space scientist; deeply connected in the US intelligence community; came into Strieber's life through Stanton Friedman; supported Strieber's work and visited his cabin.

sources.quote: Space scientist well-connected in intelligence community; supportive of Strieber

nodes.label: Uncle Mickey (Strieber)
nodes.summary: Whitley Strieber's uncle; worked at Air Material Command at Wright-Patterson in 1947 alongside General James 'Stan' Exxon during Roswell era.

sources.quote: Air Material Command 1947 insider; Strieber family pipeline

nodes.label: Communion
nodes.summary: Whitley Strieber's 1987 memoir that defined modern alien-abduction imagery; sold millions of copies; inspired a 1989 Christopher Walken film; the cover's 'Gray' illustration established the visual mythology.

nodes.label: Top Secret (Stanton Friedman)
nodes.summary: Stanton Friedman's book on the Majestic 12 leaked documents; Whitley Strieber wrote the foreword.

sources.timestamp_end: Stanton Friedman book on Majestic 12 with Strieber foreword

nodes.label: Strieber Alien Implant
nodes.summary: Alleged alien implant in Whitley Strieber's body that activates cyclically every 2-3 years with physiological heat. CT-scannable. Strieber demonstrated it on camera.

sources.timestamp_end: Alleged alien implant in Strieber's body; activates every 2-3 years

nodes.label: Per Strieber: boxes of Communion appeared in bookstores ~2 weeks before the declared publication dat
nodes.summary: Per Strieber: boxes of Communion appeared in bookstores ~2 weeks before the declared publication date without warning; his editor said 'we felt there was some kind of resistance' — unclear whether resistance from the publisher or external.

sources.quote: I was told a publication date and then all of a sudden the books were in bookstores. My editor was very coy about I said, 'What? You jumped it.' Boxes of books appeared without warning at bookstores all over the country. He said, 'Well, we felt like there was some kind of resistance.'

type_specific.statement_text: Per Strieber: boxes of Communion appeared in bookstores ~2 weeks before the declared publication date without warning; his editor said 'we felt there was some kind of resistance' — unclear whether resistance from the publisher or external.

nodes.label: Strieber has an alleged alien implant that activates every 2-3 years with physiological heat; he dem
nodes.summary: Strieber has an alleged alien implant that activates every 2-3 years with physiological heat; he demonstrates it on camera — can be touched, palpable, runs warm during active cycles. CT scan available.

sources.quote: There's something in your body that you believe is an implant. You can touch it. I feel like might be a little hot because it's been working two-three years ago. I can give you a CT scan of it.

type_specific.statement_text: Strieber has an alleged alien implant that activates every 2-3 years with physiological heat; he demonstrates it on camera — can be touched, palpable, runs warm during active cycles. CT scan available.

nodes.label: Strieber reveals: his uncle Mickey worked at Air Material Command at Wright-Patterson in 1947 alongs
nodes.summary: Strieber reveals: his uncle Mickey worked at Air Material Command at Wright-Patterson in 1947 alongside General Exxon — during the Roswell era — providing a family-pipeline explanation for why Strieber was 'chosen' for contact.

sources.quote: One of my uncles was heavily involved in this at Wright-Patt — General Exxon. No, it's my uncle Mickey and General Exxon were — worked at the Air Material Command in 47.

type_specific.statement_text: Strieber reveals: his uncle Mickey worked at Air Material Command at Wright-Patterson in 1947 alongside General Exxon — during the Roswell era — providing a family-pipeline explanation for why Strieber was 'chosen' for contact.

nodes.label: Per Strieber: space scientist David W. Webb (introduced through Stanton Friedman) was well-connected
nodes.summary: Per Strieber: space scientist David W. Webb (introduced through Stanton Friedman) was well-connected in the intelligence community and very supportive of Strieber's work — visited Strieber's cabin; the government 'was aware of this whole abduction scenario' even then.

sources.quote: I had met a space scientist David W. Webb who had come into our lives through Stanton Friedman, and he was very well-connected in the intelligence community and very supportive. So I knew the government was aware of this whole abduction scenario.

type_specific.statement_text: Per Strieber: space scientist David W. Webb (introduced through Stanton Friedman) was well-connected in the intelligence community and very supportive of Strieber's work — visited Strieber's cabin; the government 'was aware of this whole abduction scenario' even then.

nodes.label: Strieber states he knows precisely why 'they' (NHI) chose him — 'it's a family affair.' Multiple fam
nodes.summary: Strieber states he knows precisely why 'they' (NHI) chose him — 'it's a family affair.' Multiple family members with 1947-era insider positions; childhood incidents he attributes to same phenomenon.

sources.quote: I know exactly why they chose me. First, it's a family affair. One of my uncles was involved in this. My father was apparently involved.
